question_text: A sound wave has a frequency of 250 Hz and travels through air at 340 m/s. Calculate the wavelength of this sound wave. (Use: wavelength = speed ÷ frequency)
explanation: Using the wave equation: wavelength = speed ÷ frequency = 340 ÷ 250 = 1.36 m. This is a relatively long wavelength, typical of a low-frequency sound like a bass note in music.
